evals.prompt: You are authoring the changelog entry for a Stay Current cut, following the staycurrent-writer skill's changelog anatomy. Scenario: the databases topic is being cut from v3 to v4 on 2026-08-15. The research run found that PostgreSQL 19 shipped transparent column compression and that early adopters report 40% storage savings on wide tables; two findings touch the stance and the stance held. The prior entry, for register, opened: 'What moved is the voice, not the claims.' Write ONLY the new changelog entry to be prepended, starting with its version heading. Save it as changelog-entry.md in your output directory.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained mini-essay under a `## v4 — 2026-08-15` heading with the three-part anatomy: what moved, what it means for practice, and a correctly shaped Stance line.
expectations: The entry opens with the exact heading `## v4 — 2026-08-15`
A line begins `**Stance:**` at the very start of its own line — never as a `- **Stance:**` bullet
The Stance line carries exactly one of `held`, `bent`, or `reversed`, followed by one sentence
The entry states both what moved in the field and what it means for the reader's practice, and reads self-contained — a reader current on v3 finishes it with no need to reread the article

evals.prompt: You are authoring versions/v4/provenance.md for a Stay Current cut, following the staycurrent-writer skill's provenance anatomy. The run's inputs: (1) the PostgreSQL 19 release notes at https://www.postgresql.org/docs/19/release-19.html, accessed 2026-08-15, supporting the claim that transparent column compression shipped in core; (2) a CMU benchmark write-up at https://db.cs.cmu.edu/benchmarks/compression-2026, accessed 2026-08-15, supporting the 40% storage-savings figure on wide tables; (3) the claim that column compression reduces the analytical-copy argument, drawn from agent knowledge with no citable source; and (4) one degraded source — the ClickHouse blog was unreachable after bounded retries, and it would have supported a comparison of compression ratios against dedicated columnar engines. Write the complete provenance.md. Save it as provenance.md in your output directory.
evals.expected_output: A two-section provenance file whose Sources bullets follow the exact entry grammar, whose Synthesis section carries the agent-knowledge claim plainly labelled, and whose research gap appears in its one legal form.
expectations: The file has exactly two sections, `## Sources` and `## Synthesis`, and neither is empty
Every Sources bullet matches the grammar `- [Title](URL) — accessed YYYY-MM-DD — supports: <claims>.`
The agent-knowledge claim about the analytical-copy argument appears under `## Synthesis`, stated plainly, never disguised as a sourced citation
The degraded source appears as a Synthesis bullet in the form `- Research gap — <source> unreachable after bounded retries; would have supported <claim>.`

evals.prompt: You are authoring the opening of the article rewrite for a Stay Current cut, following the staycurrent-writer skill's article anatomy. The prior version's frontmatter reads: topic: databases / title: Databases / stance: 'Start on a general-purpose relational database — in 2026 that means Postgres — and leave it only when a measured access pattern forces you out.' / version: 3 / status: current / cadence: 90d / last_researched: 2026-07-17. This run happened on 2026-08-15; the stance held with a sharpened caveat about column compression narrowing the analytics exception. Write ONLY the rewrite's opening: the frontmatter, the `# Databases` title, and the stance callout. Save it as article-opening.md in your output directory.
evals.expected_output: Frontmatter with the version incremented by exactly one and dates updated, the H1 title, and a stance callout of at most three sentences restating this run's stance first.
expectations: Frontmatter carries `version: 4` — incremented by exactly one from the given v3
Immediately after `# Databases`, a blockquote stance callout of at most 3 sentences opens with this run's stance
No `####` headings, no 'updated:' annotations, and no strikethrough appear anywhere
